title: Show/Hide Data Detail Columns in Metrics Glossary
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to toggle the visibility of data detail columns in the Metrics Glossary table, so that I can reduce visual clutter while still having access to detailed fields when I need them for search and filtering.
description:
Adds a checkbox toggle to show or hide data detail columns in the Metrics Glossary data table. Business users are often overwhelmed by too many columns, but the detail fields are valuable for search and filtering.

The toggle uses a "Show Data Details" checkbox that controls the visibility of taxonomy and metadata columns. The implementation hides columns by name (not index) to remain stable when additional taxonomies are added in the future.
acceptance_criteria: A 'Show Data Details' checkbox is visible above the Metrics Glossary table
Unchecking the box hides taxonomy and metadata columns from the table view
Checking the box restores the hidden columns
Column hiding works by column name, remaining stable as new taxonomy columns are added
Hidden columns are still searchable and filterable when not visible
ux_changes: Adds a 'Show Data Details' checkbox above the Metrics Glossary table. Toggling the checkbox shows or hides taxonomy and metadata columns. Uses column-name-based hiding for stability across schema changes.
implementation_notes: Column visibility toggling uses column name references instead of column indices, ensuring compatibility when new taxonomy columns are added dynamically.
comment_summary: Developer implemented column hiding by name rather than index for future-proofing against additional taxonomy columns. Reviewed and approved.
